title: caltha palustris var. minor
intro: Caltha palustris var. minor, commonly known as marsh marigold, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the buttercup family Ranunculaceae. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Caltha palustris var. minor include its ability to reduce fever and alleviate digestive issues, making it a popular herbal remedy in traditional medicine. The plant can be prepared as an infusion, decoction, or tincture, and is often used in herbal preparations to create topical creams and ointments for skin issues.
